locations.name: 伯利兹
locations.summary: 貝里斯（英語：Belize），又稱贝里斯，香港舊譯百利士。前身为英属洪都拉斯，是中美洲东海岸的一个独立国家。伯利兹北部与墨西哥接壤，南部和西部与瓜地马拉接壤，东部濒临加勒比海。該國领土长约290公里（180英里）、宽约110公里（68英里…

locations.name: 智利
locations.summary: 智利共和国（西班牙語：República de Chile），通稱智利（西班牙語：Chile），是位於南美洲的国家，智利西部与南部濒临太平洋，北靠秘鲁，东邻玻利维亚及阿根廷。為南美洲國家聯盟的成員國。除此之外，智利与厄瓜多尔相同，是两個不…

locations.name: 哥伦比亚
locations.summary: 哥伦比亚共和国（西班牙語：República de Colombia），通稱哥倫比亞（西班牙語：Colombia），是一個位於南美洲西北部的国家，同時是拉丁美洲第四大國家，為南美洲國家聯盟的前成員國（2018年8月退出）。它的北部是加勒比…

locations.name: 哥斯达黎加
locations.summary: 哥斯达黎加共和國（西班牙語：República de Costa Rica），通稱哥斯达黎加（Costa Rica），是中美洲国家，北邻尼加拉瓜，南与巴拿马接壤，首都為聖荷西。 哥斯达黎加是当今世界上第一个裁撤军队的国家。目前哥斯大黎加是…

locations.name: 厄瓜多尔

locations.name: 萨尔瓦多

locations.name: 法屬圭亞那

locations.name: 危地马拉

locations.name: 墨西哥
locations.summary: 墨西哥合众国（西班牙語：Estados Unidos Mexicanos），通稱墨西哥（西班牙語：México），是北美洲南部的一个主权国家，北接美国，西濱太平洋，南鄰伯利兹、危地马拉，东面墨西哥湾。其领土面积达近198万平方公里，为美洲…

locations.name: Quebrada de los Cuervos
locations.summary: The Quebrada de los Cuervos (Spanish for Crows Ravine) is an important landform in Treinta y Tres Department, Uruguay.

locations.name: 里約熱內盧州
locations.summary: 里約熱內盧州（葡萄牙語：Rio de Janeiro，ˈhiw dʒi ʒʌˈnejɾu） 是巴西的一個州。 里約熱內盧州面積43,696.1km²。該州首府是1763年由葡萄牙人建立的里約熱內盧，該市也是州內最大的城市。

locations.name: 福莫萨省
locations.summary: 福爾摩沙省（西班牙語：Formosa）為阿根廷的二十三省之一，位於東北部國境處，與巴拉圭相鄰，該省首府為福莫萨市。 地名起源 該省份以及其省會之名源自於古西班牙语中的"fermosa" （現今的寫法為"hermosa"），其意为“美丽”。…

locations.name: 尤卡坦州
locations.summary: 尤卡坦州（西班牙語：Yucatán）是墨西哥的一個州，位於尤卡坦半島北部，北臨墨西哥灣。 歷史上曾包括坎佩切州和金塔納羅奧州。十九世紀時曾是尤卡坦共和国的一部份，短暫脫離墨西哥而獨立。現在是墨西哥主要的旅遊點，也是最大原住民──馬雅人的家…

locations.name: 韋拉克魯斯州
locations.summary: 韋拉克魯斯州（西班牙語：Veracruz，西班牙語發音：[beɾaˈkɾus]）是墨西哥的一個州，位於該國中部偏東，東部面向墨西哥灣。人口僅次於墨西哥州和聯邦區，居全國第三，首府哈拉帕。 正式州名為Veracruz de Ignacio…

locations.name: 潘塔納爾濕地
locations.summary: 潘塔纳尔湿地（葡萄牙語：Pantanal、西班牙語：Pantanal），位於南美洲巴西马托格罗索州及南马托格罗索州，濕地部份在玻利維亞及巴拉圭境內，總面積達242,000平方公里，是世上最大的濕地。 潘塔纳尔地勢平坦而輕微傾斜，有著曲折的…

locations.name: 新大陆鹫类
locations.summary: 新大陆鹫类（英語：New World vultures）又稱美洲鹫、新域鷲、新世界秃鹫、新大陆秃鹫，学名统称美洲鷲科（Cathartidae），包含在美洲溫帶生活的5種禿鷲及2種神鷲。除了美洲鷲屬外，所有其下的屬都是單型的。 新大陸鷲类在…

relations.subject: Lesser yellow-headed vulture
relations.evidence: Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland, swamps, mangroves, and heavily degraded former forest. It may wander over dry fields and clearings. It is not generally found in high-altitude regions. This bird with its somehow crow-like aspect gave foot to the naming of the Quebrada de los Cuervos (Crows Ravine) in Uruguay, where they dwell together with the black vulture and the turkey vulture. File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Campos dos Goytacazes - RJ, Brasil imported from iNaturalist photo 356680714.jpg|Flock, in Rio de Janeiro, Brasil File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Pilcomayo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 478244272.jpg|With a turkey vulture, in Formosa, Argentina File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Bermejo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 475887416.jpg|With a crested caracara, in Formosa, Argentina File:Vultures at Celestún Garbage Dump - Flickr - treegrow.jpg|With a black vulture, Yucatán, Mexico

relations.subject: Lesser yellow-headed vulture
relations.evidence: When flying, it travels alone and is rarely found in groups. The flight of the lesser yellow-headed is an example of static soaring flight, which uses thermals to maintain altitude without the need to flap its wings. This vulture rarely soars high in the air, preferring low altitudes. This bird is believed to be somewhat migratory in response to the changes in water level where it lives. File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Veracruz, MX imported from iNaturalist photo 31916434.jpg|Dorsal view of wings, in Veracruz, Mexico File:Lesser yellow-headed vulture (Cathartes burrovianus) in flight.JPG|Ventral view of wings, in the Pantanal, Brazil File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Bolívar, Santander, Colombia imported from iNaturalist photo 530047421.jpg|In Colombia The lesser yellow-headed vulture, like other New World vultures, has the unusual habit of urohidrosis, in which it urinates or defecates on its legs to cool them by evaporation.{{cite book |author1 = Sibley, Charles G

relations.subject: Lesser yellow-headed vulture
relations.evidence: It was considered to be the same species as the greater yellow-headed vulture until they were split in 1964. It is found in Mexico, Central America, and South America in seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland, swamps, and heavily degraded former forest
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Lesser yellow-headed vulture

relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Lesser yellow-headed vulture
relations.evidence: Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland, swamps, mangroves, and heavily degraded former forest. It may wander over dry fields and clearings. It is not generally found in high-altitude regions. This bird with its somehow crow-like aspect gave foot to the naming of the Quebrada de los Cuervos (Crows Ravine) in Uruguay, where they dwell together with the black vulture and the turkey vulture. File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Campos dos Goytacazes - RJ, Brasil imported from iNaturalist photo 356680714.jpg|Flock, in Rio de Janeiro, Brasil File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Pilcomayo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 478244272.jpg|With a turkey vulture, in Formosa, Argentina File:Lesser Yellow-headed Vulture, Bermejo, Formosa, Argentina imported from iNaturalist photo 475887416.jpg|With a crested caracara, in Formosa, Argentina File:Vultures at Celestún Garbage Dump - Flickr - treegrow.jpg|With a black vulture, Yucatán, Mexico
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。
